entries.context: F1 - which review verbs need a workstream
entries.decision: Gate INGEST only: 'lume review' / 'review emit' stays ungated (it is a pure read - gathers state, prints a protocol, writes nothing, so nothing can be orphaned); 'lume review ingest' refuses to run without a workstream to own its output - either an explicit active '-w <workstream>' or '--spawn'
entries.rationale: The orphaning problem (G2) is created exactly where files are written. Gating emit would add operator friction to a read-only verb for no integrity gain; gating ingest makes every written artifact born with a workstream that can land it.

entries.context: F2 - the spawn shape and the operator gate
entries.decision: 'lume review ingest <path> --spawn' creates a dedicated workstream (slug review-<date>-NN matching the review folder, objective derived from the result's provenance + finding counts) and attributes the review to it; it opens NO iteration - drafting and approving the first iteration (triage/adopt findings) stays with the agent + operator, so nothing self-approves. With '-w' the review is attributed to that existing active workstream instead. The review's store doc and --json output carry the owning workstream id
entries.rationale: A workstream whose iterations self-approved would route around the operator gate; spawning the container empty preserves 'the machine does not act without the operator' while guaranteeing the artifacts have a home that can commit them. Mirrors how 'lume new' leaves objective-editing and 'lume open' to the loop.

entries.context: F3 - G6 auto-capture, superseding the 0015 uniform-emit rule for review_gaps only
entries.decision: Ingest WRITES review_gaps directly as open gap records via the existing gap mechanic (status open, context carrying the review slug + why_missed + proposed_change), and drops them from the printed queue plan; proposed_workstreams and direction_decisions remain print-only behind the operator gate. Captured gaps are NOT auto-linked to any workstream (linking = triage = operator). No rerun dedupe in v0.1: each ingest run captures its own gaps (a re-ingest of the same file is a new review folder and new gap records) - noted, not hidden
entries.rationale: A gap record is mechanical capture that commissions no work - the same class as the review folder and 'lume decide', which ingest already performs ungated; print-only review_gaps demonstrably evaporate (G6's evidence). Workstreams and decisions shape direction, so they keep the gate. Rerun-dedupe needs a content identity for gaps that does not exist yet - deferred until demanded.
